parsed_review.summary: Read-only independent review of CP00-886 (RP29.P06.M04.S01-S10, risk A, 10 units: 2 security_audit + 8 implementation, "Secondary Workflow Slice" permission-matrix secondary head). The descriptor-only implementation is sound and internally consistent. The CP886 pack binding (registry.js:286), requirements/section-rows (registry.js:1435), descriptor and case set (registry.js:2716,2776), validators (validators.js:1805,1840,1897), tests (model.test.js:577-627), regenerated contracts, and the bound validation script (validate-rp29-commercial-readiness-contract.mjs) all agree with the baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json. Unit/deliverable distribution matches exactly. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced and attested: every permission-matrix row sets runtime_execution/permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/runtime_receipt_emitted/real_client_data_included/credential_or_secret_included/matched_rule_contains_secret/audit_event_body_written to false, decision_binding_descriptor is true only for the six decision-binding rows, and deny_over_allow_enforced is true only for the deny-over-allow row; the validators reject any drift from these. Authority boundary holds: descriptor authority_boundary and claude_packet keep Claude read-only and non-final-approval, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and runtime opening is deferred to human approval / CP00-887. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt:false) and control-plane validate commands, not product runtime. Review hygiene is clean (auth preflight exit 0 with valid JSON, prompt 2387<25600 bytes, Read/Grep/Glob only, summary-only correctly prohibited due to security/audit deliverables, all five required artifacts present, first review attempt). No secrets, credentials, or real client data were found in the changed contract; the one out-of-scope dirty file (progress-control-room-data.mjs) derives readiness from manifest flags and does not falsely promote CP886. Critically, the pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false/in_progress and inspection+adjudication=PENDING_REVIEW, so there is no unsupported production_ready, runtime, or enterprise-trust claim to reject. One non-blocking P3: the full deterministic validation ladder (npm test, validate, closeout-pack:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, etc.) has not yet executed — command-evidence records only 3 targeted commands (all exit 0) and explicitly defers the full ladder; this is a system-enforced post-review gate (manifest commit_policy=pending_review_and_validation), not a review defect. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Full deterministic validation ladder still pending after review (system-enforced gate, not a defect)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only three targeted commands before this hardened review (commercial_syntax_check_initial, commercial_model_service_tests_initial, rp29_commercial_validate_initial), each exit_code 0, and explicitly notes that 'full deterministic validation ladder remains required after review normalization, adjudication update, and queue regeneration.' The full ladder named in the baseline/prompt (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) has not yet been executed in evidence. This is the expected pre-review state: manifest production_ready=false/in_progress, construction-inspection and adjudication are PENDING_REVIEW, and manifest commit_policy.status=pending_review_and_validation, so nothing is over-claimed. As a read-only reviewer I cannot run these commands; I verified source consistency, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, and authority boundary statically instead.
findings.recommendation: Before promoting the commercial_readiness_permission_matrix_secondary_head_descriptor_verified flag and committing CP00-886, run the complete deterministic validation ladder and confirm every command exits 0 (and git diff --check is clean), then normalize this review receipt and update adjudication/construction-inspection accordingly. Do not promote production_ready on the basis of this review alone.
findings.post_review_disposition: tracked_non_blocking — required post-review gate already enforced by manifest commit_policy and adjudication; must pass full ladder before production_ready promotion and commit
